beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and review the day ahead.; Breakfast with the kids and light morning routine.
beats.reasoning: Derek's routine: up before sunrise on a Sunday with coffee and quiet time before the house gets busy. Low energy from yesterday (40/100) means a slower morning.

beats.activity: Drive to the baseball field for practice prep.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning: Derek coaches his kids' baseball team. Gene Coulon Park in Bellevue is a central location with good fields; travel time from Renton is ~30–40 min with light Sunday traffic.
Derek's weekend commitment: he coaches the kids' team on Sundays. Good weather (sunny, 72F) means a full morning of field work, drills, and maybe a short scrimmage.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a casual spot near the park with the kids.
beats.reasoning: Post-practice hunger and the kids want a quick meal. Chick-fil-A is close to Gene Coulon and a go-to for families.
Return to Renton after lunch; light Sunday traffic makes it a smooth ~30 min drive.

beats.activity: Afternoon downtime: kids play in the yard, Derek does some light yard work.
beats.reasoning: Sunny, warm afternoon (72F) is perfect for being outside at home. Derek's energy is still moderate, so he's keeping things low-key with yard maintenance and family time.

beats.activity: Take kids to REI for a quick errand—baseball glove maintenance or refill supplies.
beats.reasoning: Derek's familiar with REI from yesterday's trip; a quick errand to grab supplies or check on gear. Renton location keeps it local.

beats.activity: Return home; kids rest while Derek preps dinner.
beats.reasoning: Back home with 10 min buffer. Derek starts prepping for dinner—his usual routine of grilling and family meals.

beats.activity: Grill dinner and eat with family; help kids with any evening tasks.
beats.reasoning: Sunday dinner ritual: Derek grills, family eats together. Quiet, restorative time after a busy day.

diary: Sunday turned out perfect—one of those rare days where everything just clicks. Started early with coffee and the kids, then headed over to Gene Coulon to run baseball practice. The scrimmage got competitive, which I love seeing. Grabbed lunch at Chick-fil-A with the crew afterward; nothing fancy, but the kids were happy. Afternoon was mellow—let them run around the yard while I tidied things up. Made a quick stop at REI to grab some supplies, then came home to fire up the grill. The weather was flawless all day, sunny and warm. Had the family out on the patio for dinner, and for once nobody was rushing anywhere. Those moments feel rare lately. Ended the day feeling settled, like I'd actually been present.
thought_process: The calm weather set the tone early—no job site stress, no weather delays on my mind. I leaned into the coaching because it's one thing that grounds me; being useful with the kids matters more than I usually admit. The afternoon downtime wasn't forced; I just wanted to be home, do normal stuff. REI was practical but also a chance to involve the kids in taking care of their gear. By evening, I was in a good headspace—grilling, family time, no phone buzzing with construction emergencies. I think I needed this kind of Sunday. No drama, no big plans, just present. It reminded me why I push so hard during the week.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
